paragraph: Your walking speed has held remarkably steady this past week — hovering around 1.18 m/s with some nice peaks into the 1.4 range on the 24th. That consistency matters more than day-to-day variation, especially when asymmetry is staying low and step length is tracking right where it should be. Today's step count looks incomplete (341 is likely partial data), but the week overall shows solid movement patterns with your double support time staying in the high 28s to low 29s. If you're noticing fatigue creeping in, consider whether your hydration is keeping pace with the warmer weather — dehydration can quietly drag down both energy and gait mechanics before you feel thirsty.
